# 1:1 prep: Chanakya Valluri
**2026-04-22**

## Carry-forward action items

No unchecked action items from recent 1:1s with Chanakya. (Open items from other meetings are yours: Sofia Jones follow-up and recruiter form.)

## Discussion topics & questions

- [ ] Knowledge transfer status: what's left to hand off before Friday? Anything undocumented that only he knows well enough to be a risk?
- [ ] Enforced security configuration bypass PR (#427499): Tara requested changes, citing the approach is overly complicated (denylist + `internal_options`). Does he plan to simplify and land this before Friday, or should someone else pick it up?
- [ ] Stale code deletion PR: Tara flagged in the team sync that shipping a PR deleting thousands of lines right before he leaves is risky. Decide together whether to shelve it or hand it off.
- [ ] Unbundling fix PR (#428722): this is still open. Clarify whether he'll land it this week or if it needs a new owner.
- [ ] Billing budgets handoff to Nic: Chanakya was originally planned for this work. Any context or gotchas he should pass along before Nic picks it up?
- [ ] How are you feeling about the last few days? Anything you want to make sure happens before you wrap up?
- [ ] What would you change about how the team works if you were staying? He's been vocal about bottleneck shifts and end-to-end ownership. Worth capturing his honest take while there's nothing to lose.
- [ ] Feedback for me: anything I could have done better as a manager? He came from Vanguard (rotating managers) and Genesis (technical manager), so he has comparison points.
- [ ] Pizza party logistics for Friday: confirm timing, any preferences.

## Quick context

- **Last day is this Friday (April 24).** This is likely the final 1:1. Team is going from 5 to 4 engineers.
- **Still active on reviews.** Approved the INP performance PR (github-ui #17264), reviewed the unbundling fix and enterprise security page experiment this past week. Has two open PRs of his own that need resolution before he leaves.
- **Attachment pipeline ADR happened last week.** Chanakya was in the walkthrough with Steve and Tara. He has context on the planner/stager/attacher design that may be worth documenting if he hasn't already.

# 1:1 with Chanakya Valluri
**April 15, 2026**

---

## Carry-forward action items

- [x] Chanakya: Send Mike a formal resignation email stating last day (Friday April 24)
- [ ] Chanakya: Finish the security PR (fix enforced security configuration bypass via parameter injection, currently open on github/github)
- [ ] Chanakya: Switch to the big subscription cleanup work after security PR is done
- [x] Mike: Submit offboarding form in Workday once resignation email is received
- [ ] Mike: Slack Chanakya with offboarding specifics and any async follow-ups
- [ ] Mike: Plan a going-away food event for Chanakya ($25 budget, similar to Alex's orca-palooza)

## Discussion topics & questions

# Dear Big Orca: 2026-04-27

### Top of mind

It's calibration and reflections week, which means your calendar is about to look like someone played Tetris with meeting blocks and lost. Thursday's calibration session runs noon to 3pm, with feedback writing blocks on both Tuesday and Thursday afternoons. Get your reflections done early. The new template hasn't gotten less annoying, sorry.

Universe CFS + Speaker Nominations are due Thursday (May 1). Last call if you're submitting or nominating.

### Product stuff

**CSRA** is humming. We blew past 5.7K runs against the 8K corridor goal, and Catu's pushing to raise the target to 16K to match SRA adoption. Jennifer's running this well as DRI.

**Quick Setup** is in ship mode. Tara's been carrying this, and the scorecard is green after their query optimizations on the enterprise security configs page. Wednesday's Quick Setup sync at noon if you want the details.

**Sequential Enablement**: Steve's deep in the architectural exploration. He and Dorothy have a chatski Wednesday at 11:30 to work through next steps.

**Billing Budgets**: Nic is getting oriented and ramping fast. Setting up time with Lucy from licensing this week.

### People stuff

- **Chanakya** departed last Thursday. His CSRA work was solid. SE1 backfill is filed.
- **Nic** is back from vacation and asking sharp questions about billing. Ramp speed is impressive.
- **Jennifer**: CSRA DRI, cruising. Due date is September 29, so coverage planning starts soon.
- **Tara**: Quick Setup hero. 1:1 Tuesday 1pm.
- **Steve**: Sequential enablement deep dive. 1:1 Thursday 11am.
- **SE3 hiring**: Sofia Jones debrief is Tuesday at 4:30. If the interview matched the take-home, we might have our first hire locked.

### Process stuff

- Iteration Planning today at 4pm. Show up with your priorities straight.
- Ops Review Tuesday 11am.
- Security Products PLG Roadmap chat Tuesday 11:30. Big-picture product direction.
- ADR Review Wednesday 12:05. Opinions welcome.
- Eng Leads Sync Tuesday 2pm.

### Org stuff

- Ashwin 1:1 Thursday 4pm. Calibration is the main topic.
- Orcapalooza Thursday at noon. Show up, have fun, be normal.
- EPD weekly board review Thursday 10am.

See yaaaaaa @github/big-orca

weekly.title: 2026-04-skip-level-prep
weekly.content:
# Skip-level prep

## Team snapshot (as of April 2, 2026)

All five reports are on-track. No one is flagged or at risk.

| Person | Recent focus | Activity level |
|--------|-------------|----------------|
| Chanakya Valluri | Code scanning risk assessment, Safari INP perf fix, CI stability | Steady (6 PRs, 2 issues) |
| Jennifer Ramirez | Code scanning assessment end-to-end, GHAS billing fix, UI polish | High output (13 PRs, 9 issues) |
| Nic Daantos | GHAS billing edge case fix | Light (1 PR, 2 issues) |
| Steve Richert | PR stacks API, query caching perf, risk assessment onboarding | Steady (7 PRs, 4 issues) |
| Tara Kintner | GHAS SKU refactor for security configs | Light (1 PR, 1 issue) |

---

## Key themes to be aware of

**Code scanning risk assessment is the big team initiative.** Jennifer, Chanakya, and Steve are all contributing. Work spans the full stack: database schema (supported_languages column), backend jobs (timeout job, timer daemon wiring), UI (no vulnerabilities banner, scan-in-progress text, repo picker), and onboarding (`/get_started?with=` support). This is clearly the team's top priority right now.

**GHAS billing logic has a tricky bug.** Jennifer, Nic, and Steve are all touching `was_billed_more_than_30_days_ago?`, which needs to correctly handle trial-relevant GHAS SKUs across unbundling transitions. Multiple people reviewing suggests this is high-risk or nuanced.

**Performance and reliability work happening in parallel.** Steve landed a cache for `advanced_security_business_user_ids` to prevent slow query storms during mass config application. Chanakya has an open PR reducing Safari INP on security settings dropdowns. Chanakya also skipped flaky security center auth tests to unblock CI.

**Tara's refactor to the new GHAS SKU block** is a standalone effort that touches security configs broadly. Worth understanding scope and rollout plan.

---

## Possible talking points

- **Nic's workload**: Only 1 PR and 2 issues is notably lower than peers. Could be pairing, onboarding, blocked, or doing non-code work. Worth understanding context, not necessarily a concern.
- **Jennifer's volume**: 13 PRs and 9 issues is a lot. Check if she's feeling stretched or if this is a comfortable pace. She's touching billing logic, UI, backend jobs, and database changes simultaneously.
- **CI health**: Chanakya had to skip flaky tests to unblock CI. If your skip-level asks about engineering health or developer experience, this is a data point.
- **PR stacks**: Steve landed an API endpoint for PR stacks. If this is a new internal tool or workflow change, your skip-level may want to know the vision.
- **Risk assessment launch readiness**: With so many open PRs (Jennifer has 5 open), ask about timeline confidence and whether anything is blocking a ship date.

---

## Questions your skip-level might ask

1. "What's the timeline on code scanning risk assessment?" Make sure you have a clear answer or know who to point to.
2. "How's the GHAS unbundling transition going?" The billing fix suggests edge cases are still surfacing.
3. "Anyone at risk of burnout or disengagement?" Jennifer's output is high, Nic's is low. Both worth explaining.
4. "What's the team's biggest blocker right now?" Flaky CI and the billing edge case are candidates.
